response: I understand you're concerned about your test score and its impact on your job application. The scoring and evaluation of tests are handled by the platform based on the test parameters. If you believe there was an error in the grading, you would need to discuss this directly with the recruiter or the company you applied to, as they manage the hiring process.

The documentation does not provide a mechanism for support agents to alter scores or influence hiring decisions. response: HackerRank is a technology hiring platform that enables companies to efficiently hire skilled developers. For more information on setting up and using HackerRank for hiring, please refer to our Quick Start Guide for Recruiters and articles on creating tests and inviting candidates.

response: If your challenges are not loading or showing an infinite processing message, please try closing and reopening the challenge window. You can also try opening the challenge in a different supported browser like Chrome, Firefox, or Edge, and ensure your internet connection is stable. If you do not see your challenge results, please wait for them to process, as they can take some time to appear. You can also check the leaderboard, as it may take time to refresh. Finally, ensure you are logged into the same HackerRank Community account that you used to participate in the challenge, as results do not appear across different accounts.
justification: The user is experiencing issues with submissions not working and challenges not loading. The documentation provides troubleshooting steps for challenges not loading/processing and for not seeing challenge results, which are relevant to the user's reported issue.

response: You can reschedule an interview by logging into your HackerRank for Work account, navigating to the Interviews > Interview List, selecting the interview you wish to reschedule, clicking 'Invite Participants', and then updating the date and time. After updating, click 'Save and Send Email' to notify all participants of the change.
justification: The user is requesting to reschedule an assessment, which falls under the 'Interviews' product area. The documentation provides clear steps on how to reschedule an interview.

response: To remove a user from an interview template, navigate to Interviews > Templates. Click the 'More options' (⋮) menu next to the template, then select 'Share'. In the 'Share Interview Template' dialog box, select the user you wish to remove and choose 'Remove User' from the dropdown menu. Finally, click 'Confirm' in the confirmation dialog.
justification: The user is asking how to remove an interviewer, which is a type of user. The documentation provides a clear procedure for removing users from interview templates.

response: You can pause your subscription by navigating to Settings > Billing > Subscription. Click on the 'Cancel Plan' button, and you will see an option to 'Pause Subscription'. You can select a pause duration from 1 month to 12 months. If you wish to resume your subscription early, go to the Billing page and click 'Resume Plan'.
justification: The user is asking to pause their subscription, which is directly addressed in the 'Pause Subscription' documentation. The response provides instructions on how to pause and resume the subscription.

response: Ensure the Claude Desktop app was open throughout the entire task. If the app was closed or your computer went to sleep, the session may have ended.
justification: The user is reporting that Claude is not responding. Documentation states that if the Claude Desktop app is closed or the computer goes to sleep, the session may end, causing Claude to stop working on a task. This directly addresses the user's issue.

response: If your identity has been stolen and it involves your Visa card, please visit our [Lost or Stolen card](/support/consumer/lost-stolen-card.html) page. There you can learn how to cancel your card or get an emergency replacement card.

The documentation provides a specific section for 'My identity has been stolen – what should I do?' which directs the user to the 'Lost or Stolen card' page if the identity theft involves their Visa card. response: The HackerRank Resume Builder is a tool that helps you create a professional resume. You can build from scratch using a template or import an existing resume in .doc, .docx, or .pdf format. If you are experiencing issues with the Resume Builder being down, please note that some features, including the Resume Builder, are in limited availability and enabled for a small subset of users. If you would like to enable this feature for your account, please reach out to Community Support.
justification: The user is reporting an issue with the Resume Builder being down. The documentation confirms the existence of the Resume Builder and mentions that it is in limited availability for a subset of users. The documentation also provides a way to contact Community Support to enable the feature. Therefore, the response should inform the user about the limited availability and direct them to Community Support.

response: Yes, you can update the name on your certificate. You can update the name on your certificate only once per account. When you update your name, the change applies to all your certificates. After you update it, you cannot change it again. To update the name on your certificate: 1. Open your certificate page. 2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display. 3. Click Regenerate Certificate. 4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name.
justification: The user is asking to update their name on a certificate. The documentation provides clear steps on how to do this.

response: The security of our systems and user data is Anthropic’s top priority. We appreciate the work of security researchers acting in good faith in identifying and reporting potential vulnerabilities. Please review our [Responsible Disclosure Policy](https://www.anthropic.com/responsible-disclosure-policy), which includes a section on *How to Submit a Report*. Thank you very much for helping us to keep Anthropic safe.
justification: The user is reporting a security vulnerability. The documentation provides a direct link to the Responsible Disclosure Policy for submitting such reports.

response: Anthropic uses various robots to gather data from the public web for model development, to search the web, and to retrieve web content at users’ direction. To block ClaudeBot from your entire website, you can add the following to your robots.txt file in your top-level directory: User-agent: ClaudeBot Disallow: /. Please do this for every subdomain that you wish to opt out from.
justification: The user is asking how to stop Claude from crawling their website. The documentation provides specific instructions on how to do this using the robots.txt file.

response: If you need cash urgently, you can use your Visa card to withdraw local currency from ATMs worldwide that display the Visa PLUS mark. You can locate these ATMs using Visa's ATM locator. Additionally, Visa's Global Customer Assistance Services (GCAS) can provide emergency cash and card replacement services if you are abroad.
justification: The user is asking for urgent cash and mentions having a VISA card. The documentation provides information on using Visa cards for cash withdrawals at ATMs and mentions emergency cash services available through GCAS for those abroad.

response: When you allow us to use your chats or coding sessions to help improve Claude, your data is automatically de-linked from your user ID before any review. Access to this data is limited to a small number of personnel involved in model training. We use tools and processes to filter or obfuscate sensitive data, and your data is used solely to make Claude better for everyone. You maintain full control and can adjust your privacy settings at any time. Your incognito chats are not used to improve Claude, even if you have enabled Model Improvement in your Privacy Settings.
justification: The user is asking about data usage for model improvement. Chunk 1 directly addresses this by explaining the privacy protections and how data is used when users opt-in to improve Claude. It clarifies that data is de-linked, access is limited, sensitive data is filtered, and the data is used solely for model improvement, not for marketing or profiling. It also mentions that users can adjust privacy settings.

response: To set up the Claude LTI for your students, you will need to follow the steps outlined in the documentation for setting up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure. This process involves creating a Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas, installing the Claude LTI as an app, and then enabling the integration in your Claude for Education organization settings. Please refer to the following article for detailed instructions: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11725453-set-up-the-claude-lti-in-canvas-by-instructure. If you have any questions about your Claude for Education plan account or the Claude LTI, we encourage you to contact your university’s administrator(s).
justification: The user is asking how to set up a Claude LTI key for their students, which is directly covered by the documentation on setting up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure. The response provides a summary of the steps and a link to the relevant article.

response: In general, a merchant is not permitted to establish a minimum or maximum amount for a Visa transaction. However, exceptions apply in the USA and US territories – like Puerto Rico, the US Virgin Islands and Guam. In those locations, and only for credit cards, a merchant may require a minimum transaction amount of US$10. If you encounter an issue where a merchant has refused to accept your Visa card on the basis that the merchant requires a minimum or maximum amount on a Visa debit card, or requires that the purchase amount on a credit card is greater than US$10, please notify your Visa card issuer.
justification: The user is asking about a merchant setting a minimum spend limit on their Visa card in the US Virgin Islands. Chunk 1 directly addresses this scenario, explaining the exception for US territories and advising the user to contact their card issuer if the issue involves a debit card or a credit card purchase below $10.
